{{Quote|For all practical purposes, the war is over, sire...|[[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]] to [[Roan Fel]]|<ref name="Legacy 8" />}}
The '''Battle of Caamas''' was the last major battle between the [[Fel Empire]] and the [[Galactic Federation of Free Alliances|Galactic Alliance]] in the [[Sith–Imperial War]].

==Prelude==
{{Quote|If we can capture or kill Fel, we can force a peace and end this war! We must go with all available ships and that includes the core fleet|Rear Admiral Piers Petan|<ref name="Legacy 20" />}}
The Galactic Alliance was fed intel that indicated [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Roan Fel]] would be present at the planet's orbit to discuss an alliance with envoys of the [[Chiss/Legends|Chiss]] [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Ascendancy]] and the Hapans and be protected only by a light escort. The newly promoted [[Rear Admiral/Legends|Rear Admiral]] [[Piers Petan]] advocated moving the core forces of the [[Galactic Alliance Defense Fleet]] in order to capture or kill Emperor Fel. This plan was supported by [[Nu Toreena]], an [[Ithorian/Legends|Ithorian]] member of the [[Triumvirate (Galactic Alliance)|Triumvirate]], while it was opposed by [[Mon Calamari/Legends|Mon Calamari]] Triumvirate [[Gial Gahan]], [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Gar Stazi, and [[New Jedi Order]] representative [[Kol Skywalker]], who argued the [[Jedi Council/Legends|Jedi Council]] sensed a trap. The only undecided member of the Triumvirate was [[Bail Antilles (Galactic Alliance)|Bail Antilles]].

However, Petan's argument that bold action was necessary, in the face of the many systems declaring neutrality or joining the Empire, convinced Antilles. Even though Stazi agreed to move the Core Fleet, he ordered his aide, communications officer [[Jaius Yorub]] to prepare an escape route in case the Alliance was defeated.

==The battle==
[[File:Breakout.jpg|thumb|left|200px|The remnants of the [[Core Fleet|Alliance Core Fleet]] attempt a breakout.]]
{{Quote|I will surrender, Admiral, when I can no longer fight!|Alliance Admiral Gar Stazi, to Imperial Grand Admiral Morlish Veed|<ref name="Legacy 21" />}}
Over [[Caamas/Legends|Caamas]], the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial fleet]] under the command [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Morlish Veed]] and his flagship [[Imperious (Pellaeon-class)|''Imperious'']] decimated the Alliance fleet, while the [[Resolute (Fel Empire)|''Resolute'']], under the command of [[Edouard Fenel]], targeted enemy ships too damaged to properly defend themselves. The New Jedi Order could not aid the Alliance in the battle, due to a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Battle of Coruscant (Sith–Imperial War)|feint]] near [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. This attack cost the Jedi Order heavy losses, as well as depriving the Alliance of badly needed aid.

Late in the battle, the Alliance [[Supreme Commander (Alliance)/Legends|Supreme Commander]], Rear Admiral Petan, accepted an offer to surrender from [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Morlish Veed]]. [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Gar Stazi]], who refused to accept Veed's offer, led his remaining forces into a fighting withdrawal, thought impossible because the Imperials had mined the nearby [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] with [[gravity bomb]]s and [[Space mine/Legends|space mines]]. Stazi's lead was followed by other Alliance units, including the ''[[Pride of Corellia]]'' and it's commander, [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Nict]]. Unfortunately, the ''Pride of Corellia'' was destroyed moments before the fleet jumped into hyperspace using secret spacer routes.

==Aftermath==
{{Quote|…Coruscant can no longer be militarily defended. The Galactic Alliance must now surrender.|[[Morrigan Corde|Nyna Calixte]] to [[Roan Fel]]|<ref name="Legacy 8" />}}
Following this battle, [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] had no viable defenses and was subjugated by the Empire. Emperor Fel tried to convince the remaining Jedi on Coruscant to join his [[Imperial Knight]]s. While some Jedi accepted, others fled to Ossus, where they would be [[Massacre at Ossus|hunted down]] by the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] and the [[1st Imperial Center Core Defense Squadron|forces]] commanded by [[Rulf Yage]].

Meanwhile, as a result of his victory, Grand Admiral Veed was promoted to the rank [[High Moff]]. And on the other side of the conflict, Stazi's escape and subsequent [[Second Imperial Civil War|guerilla war]] made him a legend among the Imperials, though [[Dru Valan]] would be blamed for allowing Stazi to flee.
